title: RV8GR ALU, accumulator, and Z flag
purpose: Prove the RV8GR accumulator datapath: ADD/SUB/XOR/LI result selection, AC edge capture, AC store buffer, and Z flag update timing.

chips.role: AC output buffer onto IBUS for store

behavior.xor_b: XOR_MODE=0 selects {8{ALU_SUB}}. XOR_MODE=1 selects AC feedback.
behavior.adder: SUM = AC + XOR_Y + ALU_SUB. With XOR_MODE=0 this gives ADD or two's-complement SUB.
behavior.ac_mux: MUX_SEL=0 selects SUM. MUX_SEL=1 selects XOR_Y.
behavior.li: LI works with XOR_MODE=0, ALU_SUB=0, MUX_SEL=1, so AC receives IBUS.
behavior.accumulator: U9 captures the selected result on ACC_CLK rising. ACC_CLK is valid only during T2 when AC_WR=1.
behavior.z_flag: Z is cleared by the ACC_CLK edge and then U22.Y asynchronously presets it when the new AC equals zero. U22.Y is active LOW when AC equals zero.
behavior.ac_buffer: U14 may drive AC onto IBUS only when /AC_BUF is LOW during store.

trigger_edges: U9 captures on ACC_CLK rising edge
U21 updates Z on the same ACC_CLK edge plus U22 async preset when AC=0
propagation_checks: xor_b_to_ac_d_model_path: 74HC157 + 74HC86 + two 74HC283 stages + 74HC157 = 121 ns model delay
unsafe_states: AC feedback treated as a combinational loop instead of registered feedback
Z sampled before the comparator/preset path settles

guarantees: ADD, SUB, XOR, and LI datapath equations match RV8GR RTL and opcode sweep
AC captures only when T2 and AC_WR create ACC_CLK
No-edge and non-AC_WR cases hold AC
Z flag follows the captured AC value
U14 store buffer drives IBUS only when enabled
Clock profiles cover manual push, random 100 pushes, 50 kHz, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz functional simulation

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
